page.title: Easter Monday Yacht Charter in Marbella: A Sunny Holiday Escape

data.meta_description: Escape the crowds with an Easter Monday yacht charter in Marbella. Sunny skies, calm seas, and boats from €749/2h. Book your holiday escape now.
data.summary: Easter Monday in Marbella is perfect for a yacht charter: warm spring weather, calm Mediterranean waters, and exclusive boats from Puerto Banús. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, drinks, and taxes.
key_facts: Easter Monday charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella.
Spring sea temperatures average 16°C in April.
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and IVA.
Mangusta 80 (24m) comes with a free jet ski on 4h+ bookings.
Popular destinations: Cala del Faro, Cabopino, and Estepona coast.
Sunset cruises are especially popular on Easter Monday evenings.
Book early – Easter Monday is a peak holiday date.

faq.q: What is the best boat for an Easter Monday yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: For small groups (up to 9 pax), the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) is ideal. For larger parties (up to 12), the Mangusta 80 or Azimut 58 Flybridge offer more space and a free jet ski on 4h+ bookings.

faq.q: How much does an Easter Monday yacht charter in Marbella cost?
faq.a: Prices start at €749 for a 2-hour charter on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39. The Mangusta 80 has a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on board?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The standard package already includes a selection of beverages and snacks.

faq.q: What destinations can we visit on an Easter Monday charter?
faq.a: Popular routes include cruising along the Golden Mile to Cala del Faro, anchoring at Cabopino, or heading west towards Estepona and Sotogrande. Your skipper will tailor the itinerary.

faq.q: Is Easter Monday a good time for a yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Absolutely. April offers mild temperatures (18–22°C), calm seas, and fewer tourists than summer. The spring light is beautiful, making it perfect for photography and relaxation.

faq.q: How do I book an Easter Monday yacht charter?
faq.a: You can book directly on our website or contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance for Easter Monday.

faq.q: What is the cancellation policy for Easter Monday charters?
faq.a: Cancellations made 72 hours before departure receive a full refund. Within 72 hours, a 50% charge applies. No-shows are non-refundable.

data.body_html:
Easter Monday in Marbella is a glorious affair: the crowds from Semana Santa thin out, the sun warms the Costa del Sol to a pleasant 22°C, and the Mediterranean lies flat as a mirror. An Easter Monday yacht charter in Marbella offers the best way to celebrate the holiday – away from packed beaches, with your own private deck, cold cava, and the freedom to anchor wherever you please. Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús, and prices start at €749 for two hours, all inclusive.

Why Choose an Easter Monday Yacht Charter in Marbella?

Easter Monday (Lunes de Pascua) is a public holiday across Spain, and Marbella locals flock to the coast. Rather than queuing for a sunbed, you can skip the crowds entirely. A private charter gives you access to secluded coves like Cala del Faro (2 NM west of Puerto Banús) or the sandy stretch at Cabopino (4 NM east). The spring light – low and golden – makes for exceptional photos of La Concha mountain in the background. With sea temperatures around 16°C, swimming is brisk but invigorating.

Our Recommended Boats for Easter Monday

For a small group or couple, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are perfect. Both are priced at €749 for 2 hours, €1,299 for 4 hours, or €2,299 for a full 8-hour day. For a larger party, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) – available in grey or white – offers a minimum 4-hour charter at €4,719, and includes a free jet ski to tow behind. Other boats like the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) or Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) are available on request via WhatsApp.

What’s Included in Your Easter Monday Charter

Every booking comes with a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, and a drinks package: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. Light snacks are also provided. Spanish IVA (21%) is included in all prices – no surprises at checkout. You are free to bring extra food or drinks aboard.

Sample Itinerary: 4-Hour Easter Monday Cruise

  • 12:00 – Depart Puerto Banús, head west past the Golden Mile villas.
  • 12:30 – Anchor at Cala del Faro for swimming and snacks.
  • 13:30 – Cruise towards Estepona, spotting dolphins en route.
  • 14:30 – Stop at a quiet bay near Río Verde for lunch and cava.
  • 15:30 – Return to port, enjoying the afternoon sun.

Longer 8-hour charters can extend to Sotogrande or Cabopino.

Easter Monday Weather and Sea Conditions

April in Marbella averages 18–22°C air temperature, with 9 hours of sunshine. The sea is typically calm (swell under 0.5m), making it ideal for first-time boaters. Light jacket recommended for the evening return. Wind is usually light from the west at 5–10 knots.

How to Book Your Easter Monday Yacht Charter

Visit our main page to browse the full fleet, or contact us directly via WhatsApp for pricing and availability on boats not listed with fixed rates. Easter Monday is one of the busiest dates of the year – we recommend booking at least two weeks in advance. For a quick option, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 at €749/2h are often still available last-minute.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best boat for an Easter Monday yacht charter in Marbella?

For small groups (up to 9 pax), the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) is ideal. For larger parties (up to 12), the Mangusta 80 or Azimut 58 Flybridge offer more space and a free jet ski on 4h+ bookings.

How much does an Easter Monday yacht charter in Marbella cost?

Prices start at €749 for a 2-hour charter on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39. The Mangusta 80 has a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees.

Can I bring my own food and drinks on board?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The standard package already includes a selection of beverages and snacks.

What destinations can we visit on an Easter Monday charter?

Popular routes include cruising along the Golden Mile to Cala del Faro, anchoring at Cabopino, or heading west towards Estepona and Sotogrande. Your skipper will tailor the itinerary.

Is Easter Monday a good time for a yacht charter in Marbella?

Absolutely. April offers mild temperatures (18–22°C), calm seas, and fewer tourists than summer. The spring light is beautiful, making it perfect for photography and relaxation.

How do I book an Easter Monday yacht charter?

You can book directly on our website or contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance for Easter Monday.

What is the cancellation policy for Easter Monday charters?

Cancellations made 72 hours before departure receive a full refund. Within 72 hours, a 50% charge applies. No-shows are non-refundable.

Don’t let Easter Monday pass you by on a crowded beach. Reserve your yacht charter in Marbella today and enjoy a sunny holiday escape on the Mediterranean.
